title: Asthma Care Quick Reference
description: A comprehensive guide on asthma management, including medication information, patient education, and resources.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: This document provides essential information on asthma management, focusing on inhaled corticosteroids and long-acting beta-agonists.

subsections.title: Inhaled Corticosteroids (ICS)
subsections.content: ICSs are the cornerstone of asthma treatment, effective for long-term control by reducing airway inflammation.

subsections.title: Long-Acting Beta-Agonists (LABAs)
subsections.content: LABAs are used in combination with ICSs for better asthma control, but carry risks if used alone.

sections.title: Patient Education
sections.content: Key points for patients include daily use of ICSs, proper technique, and understanding the difference between ICSs and other steroids.

sections.title: Resources
sections.content: Provides links to national guidelines and organizations for further information on asthma management.

author: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute
